text:
Athens Times

—

Persona non grata is the one for activists against sects who condemned the possible appearance of the actor and member of Scientology, at the Paris closing ceremony on Sunday, August 11. Although Tom Cruz’s presence has not been confirmed, Mission Impossible star is expected to participate in the final show of the Olympic Games, infuriating a French umbrella group for the victims of sects. “The fact that we are talking about his presence is an insult to the victims,” said Catherine Katz, a former French judge who heads UNADFI, a group dedicated to defending the victims of sects, as Le Monde writes. “It’s really a bad message”. Charlene Delport, president of the Caffes victims’ association, spoke of “abomination”. Activists, such as Katz and Delporte, accused Scientologists of recruiting outside the Olympic facilities, including Stade de France, which will host the closing ceremony. “They are present in the major sporting events, they will undoubtedly approach many young people, I am very concerned,” Delporte said. Red flag of Scientologists Miviludes, a French government agency responsible for dealing with heretical movements, said that she has received reports of pamphlets under the slogan “No drugs” associated with Scientology and distributed throughout Paris. While the 30-page pamphlets do not explicitly mention the Church of Scientology, the campaign is linked to it through an organization funded by Scientology, the Foundation for a drug-free world, Katz said. “These are puppets,” Katz told AFP. “If you don’t know who they are, you can fall on their grand values. They say they are here to help drug addicts, but they actually allow indoctrination.” The leaflets, according to Miviludes, even appeared in some Paris pharmacies. “We are concerned that this could be a proselytist approach that has nothing to do with prevention,” said Donatien Le Vaillant’s chief at AFP. The agency warned about the “risks of psychological destabilization, excessive economic expectations” and the separation of people from their families and friends associated with Scientology, whose members have previously been convicted of fraud in France. The movement opened in April a center in St Denis, where the Stade de France is located. Scientology was created by American writer L. Ron Hubbard (1911–1986) in May 1952. Initially Hubbard created a program of ideas known as mental, whose documents and materials were distributed by the Mentalism Foundation. The foundation was soon bankrupted and Hubbard lost rights to the Dianetics project: The Modern Science of Mental Health (Dental Health): The Modern Science of Mental Health) in 1952. Hubbard re-charted the subject of intellectual in religion and renamed it Scientology, maintaining terminology, doctrines, electronic measure (E-meter) and the practice of auditing. Within a year, he regained rights over intellectual works and retained the two subjects under the umbrella of the Church of Scientology. The Church of Scientology says that man is an immortal, spiritual being (thetan) who inhabits a physical body. The thetan had countless past lives and according to the advanced texts of scientology the lives that preceded the coming of the thetan to Earth were lived in alien cultures. From research carried out at advanced levels, it is predicted that every scientologist under scrutiny will be able to remember a common series of events.

Athens Times

News about Greece and the World.
title: Olympic Games: Tom Cruz is unwanted at the closing ceremony, say anti-domination activists - Athens Times
author: uploader
